meanings.definition: Shook suddenly and briefly because of fear, cold, shock, disgust, or strong emotion.
examples: She shuddered when she heard the strange noise outside.
The child was shuddering from the freezing wind.
He shudders whenever he remembers the accident.
The entire crowd shuddered at the terrifying scene.

meanings.definition: Moved or vibrated suddenly with a shaking motion.
examples: The old train shuddered before coming to a stop.
Windows shuddered during the thunderstorm.
The engine was shuddering violently on the rough road.
The building briefly shuddered after the explosion.

meanings.definition: A sudden shaking movement caused by fear, emotion, cold, or shock.
examples: A shudder ran through her body after the nightmare.
He felt a sudden shudder of fear.
The loud scream sent shudders through the audience.
She gave a small shudder and stepped back.

meanings.definition: Writing something down quickly in a short or informal way.
examples: She was jotting notes during the lecture.
He jotted the phone number on a piece of paper.
Students often jot down important points while studying.
I quickly jotted a reminder before leaving.

meanings.definition: A short note or quick piece of writing made to remember something.
examples: Her notebook was full of random jottings.
He checked his jottings before the meeting began.
The writer kept small jottings of story ideas.
Several hurried jottings covered the page.

meanings.definition: To trip or lose balance while walking or moving.
examples: He stumbled over a rock on the dark path.
The tired runner was stumbling near the finish line.
She stumbles whenever she walks in high heels.
The child stumbled but quickly stood up again.

meanings.definition: To speak or act in an uncertain, confused, or awkward way.
examples: He stumbled through his speech nervously.
She was stumbling over difficult words while reading.
The witness stumbled when answering questions.
He stumbled into an explanation that made little sense.

meanings.definition: To accidentally discover or encounter something.
examples: They stumbled upon an old cave in the forest.
She stumbled across a rare book in the market.
Researchers stumbled on a surprising discovery.
I accidentally stumbled into the wrong classroom.

meanings.definition: A loss of balance, mistake, or moment of difficulty.
examples: One stumble nearly cost him the race.
The company recovered quickly after its financial stumble.
Her small stumble during the dance went unnoticed.
Every learner makes a few stumbles in the beginning.

meanings.definition: Hit the side of something with great force, especially in a vehicle accident.
examples: The truck broadsided the small car at the crossing.
A speeding vehicle had broadsided the bus during the storm.
The cyclist was nearly broadsided by a turning van.
The ship was broadsided by huge waves.

meanings.definition: Strongly criticized, attacked, or shocked someone suddenly with words or actions.
examples: The journalist broadsided the minister during the interview.
She was broadsided by unexpected criticism from her team.
The report broadsides several powerful companies.
He felt emotionally broadsided by the sudden announcement.

meanings.definition: A strong attack, criticism, or forceful hit directed at someone or something.
examples: The speech was a direct broadside against corruption.
Critics launched broadsides at the new policy.
The newspaper published a political broadside.
The army suffered a broadside from enemy fire.
